rationale: The query's engine is a Turing-instability analysis of coupled activator-inhibitor variables on a network: linear stability about a homogeneous state, disturbances decomposed onto graph-Laplacian eigenmodes, yielding a discrete dispersion relation, degree-dependent differentiation, and a mean-field prediction (mirrored exactly in hyp_abstract's lake phosphorus/oxygen model). The candidate instead reconstructs and characterizes the C. elegans connectome's static topology (degree distributions, synaptic multiplicities, small-world/motif structure) and separately simulates signal propagation via generic 'linear systems theory' — there is no activator-inhibitor coupling, no bifurcation/instability analysis, and no Laplacian-eigenmode dispersion relation, so the machinery does not match despite the shared word 'linear'. The object is nonetheless genuinely different from the query's abstract network-dynamics substrate: it is a specific biological wiring diagram (neurons and synapses), not lakes/basins or any coupled-field network akin to the hypothetical, so object_difference is true even though method_coherence fails.
